title: Performance Management
description: Your best people don't leave because of money — they leave because of frustration, unclear expectations, and no career path. And poor performance can't simply be dismissed without a documented process — South Africa's Labour Relations Act treats performance dismissals as incapacity, requiring counselling, a Performance Improvement Plan, monitoring, and an incapacity enquiry before any dismissal. OptiHR builds practical performance management systems that improve results, retain top performers, and protect your business when performance dismissal becomes necessary.

whyOptiHR.title: KPI development & performance systems
whyOptiHR.subtitle: clear goals, measurable results
whyOptiHR.body: We develop clear, measurable KPIs for every role tied to your business objectives — no vague targets, no guessing. We build complete performance management systems including goal-setting, regular check-ins, appraisal processes, feedback mechanisms, development plans, and documented improvement processes your managers will actually use.

whyOptiHR.title: Performance Improvement Plans
whyOptiHR.subtitle: legally compliant, CCMA-ready
whyOptiHR.body: South African labour law requires you to prove the employee knew the required standards, was given support and training, had reasonable time to improve, and was warned about dismissal before a performance dismissal can stand at the CCMA. We create legally compliant PIPs that document underperformance, set clear targets, provide support structures, and establish timelines — protecting you if dismissal becomes necessary.

whyOptiHR.title: Retention strategy & career pathing
whyOptiHR.subtitle: keep the people worth keeping
whyOptiHR.body: We identify why people leave, what keeps them engaged, and build retention strategies tailored to your workforce. We create clear career progression paths so employees see a future with you — not with your competitors. Combined with fair reward systems that recognise high performance, retention improves measurably.

legalConsiderations.title: Labour Relations Act — Incapacity
legalConsiderations.subtitle: poor performance requires a fair process.
legalConsiderations.body: Poor performance is treated as incapacity under the LRA, not misconduct. This requires counselling, a documented Performance Improvement Plan, reasonable time and support to improve, and an incapacity enquiry before dismissal. Without documented performance management, a performance dismissal will be declared unfair at the CCMA.

legalConsiderations.title: Code of Good Practice: Dismissal (2025)
legalConsiderations.subtitle: incapacity enquiry requirements.
legalConsiderations.body: The 2025 Code of Good Practice provides detailed guidance on performance dismissals — including what must be proven, the role of counselling and support, and the incapacity enquiry process. Following this process correctly is essential for any performance-related dismissal to survive CCMA scrutiny.

faq.question: Can we dismiss an employee who is just not performing?
faq.answer: Yes, but only after following the correct process. South African labour law treats poor performance as incapacity — not misconduct. Before dismissal, you must show the employee knew the required performance standards, was given coaching and support, had a reasonable period to improve with a Performance Improvement Plan, was warned that failure to improve could lead to dismissal, and was given a fair incapacity enquiry. Skip any step and the CCMA will likely find the dismissal unfair.

faq.question: What is a Performance Improvement Plan and why do we need one?
faq.answer: A PIP is a documented process that sets clear performance targets, specifies the support provided to the employee, establishes a reasonable timeframe for improvement, and records whether improvement was achieved. Without a PIP, you cannot prove you followed the correct process for a performance dismissal. A well-drafted PIP also motivates employees to improve — many underperformers respond positively to clear expectations and structured support.

faq.question: How do we retain top performers in a competitive market?
faq.answer: Retention strategies for South African businesses focus on: clear career progression paths showing employees where they're heading, fair and transparent pay structures, regular recognition of performance, flexible working arrangements where possible, professional development investment, and management quality. People stay for great managers and clear futures — they leave because of poor management and stagnation.

faq.question: What is an incapacity enquiry?
faq.answer: An incapacity enquiry is a formal hearing conducted before dismissal for poor performance — similar to a disciplinary hearing but focused on whether the employee can or cannot meet the required performance standard. The enquiry assesses whether the performance standard is reasonable, whether the employee was given proper support, whether they are capable of improvement, and whether dismissal is an appropriate outcome. Getting this right is essential for the dismissal to hold at the CCMA.

faqBody: Questions we hear from managers and business owners about managing performance legally and effectively in South Africa.

sections.text: Performance management is the system that turns vague expectations into clear, measurable goals called KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) . Employee retention is the strategy that keeps your best people from walking out the door . Without both, you're constantly hiring, training, and losing money .
Why performance management isn't optional—it's legally required:

You cannot dismiss someone just because you feel they're underperforming — South Africa's Labour Relations Act requires you to follow a fair process before dismissing for poor performance . Without documented performance management, your dismissal will be declared unfair at the CCMA, costing you reinstatement or compensation .

Performance dismissals require proof — You must show the employee knew the required standards, was given support and training, had reasonable time to improve, and was warned about dismissal . No performance management system = no proof = unfair dismissal .

This is an incapacity dismissal, not misconduct — Poor performance is treated as incapacity under the LRA, requiring counseling, monitoring, and an incapacity enquiry (hearing) before dismissal . You can't just fire someone—you must manage the process.
What we do:

KPI Development — We work with you to create clear, measurable performance indicators for every role that link to your business goals . No vague targets. No guessing. Just concrete measures everyone understands .

Performance Systems Design — We build practical performance management systems that include goal-setting, regular check-ins, performance reviews, feedback mechanisms, development plans, and documented performance improvement processes .

Performance Improvement Plans (PIPs) — We create legally compliant PIPs that document underperformance, set clear improvement targets, provide support, and establish timelines—protecting you if dismissal becomes necessary .

Incapacity Enquiry Preparation — If performance doesn't improve, we prepare you for the required incapacity enquiry (hearing), ensuring procedural fairness and documentation that will hold up at the CCMA .

Manager Training — We train your managers to have performance conversations, give constructive feedback, identify underperformance early, document interventions, and follow fair dismissal procedures for poor performance .
Retention Strategy Development — We identify why people leave, what keeps them engaged, and build retention strategies tailored to your South African workforce .
Career Pathing — We help you create clear career progression paths so employees see a future with you, not with your competitors .
Recognition & Reward Systems — We design fair, motivating reward systems that recognize high performance and drive results .
What you get:
Clear KPIs for every role tied to business objectives
A practical performance management system your managers will actually use
Legally compliant performance improvement processes protecting you from unfair dismissal claims
Trained managers who know how to manage performance effectively and legally
Lower turnover—your best people stay
Higher engagement—staff know what's expected and how they're doing
Better results—performance improves when it's measured and managed
Businesses in South Africa with high staff turnover losing good people to competitors
Companies where employees don't know what's expected of them or how they're measured
Organizations in Gauteng and across South Africa with performance management systems that exist on paper but aren't used
Employers who have dismissed for poor performance without proper processes and now face CCMA claims
Growing businesses needing to formalize performance management as they scale
Managers struggling to have difficult performance conversations or address underperformance
Private schools wanting to improve educator performance and retention
Companies with disengaged staff showing low morale and productivity
Businesses preparing for Employment Equity audits needing documented performance processes
We build systems that work in reality, not just on paper — Your existing content highlights practical implementation. We design performance systems managers will actually use, not file away .

Legal compliance built in — Our owner is an admitted attorney. Your performance management system will comply with South Africa's Labour Relations Act, Code of Good Practice: Dismissal, and protect you from unfair dismissal claims at the CCMA .
We make performance dismissals defensible — If you need to dismiss for poor performance, we ensure you have the documented evidence, fair process, and legal procedures to win at the CCMA .
We focus on retention, not just performance — Performance management that doesn't consider engagement drives people away. We integrate retention strategies into performance systems .
Manager training included — Systems fail when managers don't know how to use them. We train your team to manage performance confidently and legally .
We measure what matters — No vanity metrics. We help you identify KPIs that actually drive business results in the South African market .
